reasoning: A reasonable person would immediately agree that saving a 6-year-old patient's life outweighs the obligation to observe a picket line. The imminent risk of death makes the union's refusal to grant an exception obviously unreasonable.

A reasonable person would immediately agree that preventing an active fire hazard or falling object takes precedence over union jurisdictional rules, particularly when the authorized personnel is unreachable. The immediate threat to physical safety makes the exception obviously warranted.
